headline: Assessing CrowdStrike (CRWD) Valuation After Sharp Multi‑Year Share Price Gains
summary: CrowdStrike Holdings (CRWD) has drawn investor attention recently, with the stock last closing at US$782.17 and the company reporting annual revenue of US$4.8b alongside a net loss of US$162.5m. headline: Cognizant and CrowdStrike Expand Strategic Alliance to Secure the Agentic Enterprise
summary: Cognizant (NASDAQ: CTSH) announced an expanded strategic alliance with CrowdStrike to help enterprises secure artificial intelligence across its lifecycle, from the AI agents and models to the foundational infrastructure that supports the entire AI ecosystem. Building on a partnership established in 2025, Cognizant is bringing the CrowdStrike Falcon® platform to its AI Factory and its Managed Cybersecurity Services, powered by the Cognizant Neuro® Cybersecurity platform.

content.title: Cognizant and CrowdStrike Expand Strategic Alliance to Secure the Agentic Enterprise
content.summary: Cognizant (NASDAQ: CTSH) announced an expanded strategic alliance with CrowdStrike to help enterprises secure artificial intelligence across its lifecycle, from the AI agents and models to the foundational infrastructure that supports the entire AI ecosystem. Building on a partnership established in 2025, Cognizant is bringing the CrowdStrike Falcon® platform to its AI Factory and its Managed Cybersecurity Services, powered by the Cognizant Neuro® Cybersecurity platform.

headline: Palo Alto Earnings Just Proved AI Skeptics Wrong About Cybersecurity
summary: Palo Alto Networks  reported strong third-quarter earnings results on Tuesday afternoon, beating estimates for every major metric.  Amid skepticism about artificial-intelligence disruption of software companies, the results reinforce the argument Palo Alto and its peers have made: AI creates more work for cybersecurity firms, not less.  Adjusted earnings per share were 85 cents, ahead of Wall Street’s consensus estimate of 80 cents, and up from 80 cents last year.

content.title: Palo Alto Earnings Just Proved AI Skeptics Wrong About Cybersecurity
content.summary: Palo Alto Networks  reported strong third-quarter earnings results on Tuesday afternoon, beating estimates for every major metric.  Amid skepticism about artificial-intelligence disruption of software companies, the results reinforce the argument Palo Alto and its peers have made: AI creates more work for cybersecurity firms, not less.  Adjusted earnings per share were 85 cents, ahead of Wall Street’s consensus estimate of 80 cents, and up from 80 cents last year.
